methodsAttempted.notes: Returned mostly official UM eJournal AI manuscript policies; rejected journal-only sources as not university-wide student or teaching guidance.
Found official SPM policy and student guidance pages plus an IAS-hosted policy PDF.
Confirmed the SPM student guidance and policy pages discuss student AI use and assessment.
Confirmed official teaching and learning guidance; research and forum pages were not used.
Found the official IAS PDF compilation and rejected journal-only AI policy pages.
Found the official student declaration PDF and unrelated project pages.
Did not find separate university-wide privacy guidance beyond the official AI policy PDF and SPM policy page.
Confirmed official SPM pages are the strongest public sources; third-party and social results were not used.
Returned the official SPM announcement and third-party results; announcement retained only as a rejected news-only source.
Confirmed official SPM student guidance and policy pages for assessment-specific AI use.

artifacts.summary: Firecrawl discovery found official SPM AI policy and student guidance pages plus an IAS-hosted policy PDF. Journal-only, research-only, and announcement-only pages were rejected and do not support approved claims.

stopConditions: Stop after verified official policy, student guidance, and official PDF sources are fetched.
Do not use rejected news-only, journal-only, research-only, login-gated, or third-party pages as claim support.
Escalate to Playwright only if Firecrawl and HTTP fetch fail on official pages; no Playwright escalation was required.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Teaching and Learning
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: AI should support learning rather than replace a student’s own thinking or analysis. Students remain responsible for ensuring that all submitted work reflects their own understanding and intellectual contribution.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Teaching and Learning
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Teaching and Learning
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Lecturers will specify the level of AI use permitted for each assignment or assessment. These may include: Level 0 – No AI use: AI tools are not permitted. Level 1 – Minimal use: Basic tools such as grammar or spelling checkers may be allowed. Level 2 – Limited use: AI may assist with idea generation or concept clarification. Level 3 – Open use: AI may support learning and content development with proper declaration. Level 4 – Integrated use: AI may be required as part of the assessment activity.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Levels of AI Use in Coursework
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Teaching and Learning
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Teaching and Learning
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Students are required to: Declare any AI tools used in their assignments or assessments Explain how the AI tool was used (e.g., brainstorming, summarising, or analysis) Provide logs or evidence of AI usage if requested by the lecturer Failure to disclose AI use may be considered academic misconduct.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Teaching and Learning
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Teaching and Learning
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Students should be cautious when using public AI platforms such as generative AI tools. Information submitted to these platforms may not remain confidential. Students must not upload confidential academic materials, research data, or university documents to public AI platforms without permission from their lecturer or supervisor.

artifacts.title: Universiti Malaya staged AI policy crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged crawl for Universiti Malaya (University of Malaya, UM; QS 2026 rank =58; Malaysia) found official English-language UM sources for AI use in teaching, learning, assessment, research work, declaration, and confidentiality/privacy-risk guidance. All claims are staged candidates only and require human review before canonical publication.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This staged run does not publish canonical claims and does not mark any item human_reviewed.
No Malay-language source was needed because official English sources directly supported the staged claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
